ext_name.description: Extension name shown in the Chrome Web Store and management UI.

ext_description.message: Route only this browser's traffic through a WireGuard tunnel (via the wireproxy userspace client), without affecting the rest of the system.
ext_description.description: Extension description for the Chrome Web Store (max 132 chars).

status_disconnected.description: Badge text when the tunnel is off.

status_connected.description: Badge text when the tunnel is on.

no_profiles.description: Placeholder option in the profile selector when none exist.

btn_configure.description: Footer link to the options page.

meta_socks.description: Local proxy address shown when connected.

error_no_host.description: Generic error when the native host fails to respond.

host_missing_title.description: Heading shown in the popup when the native host is missing.

host_missing_desc.message: WireGuardExt needs a small companion app to route traffic. Install it, then reload this popup.
host_missing_desc.description: Explanation shown when the native host is missing.

host_missing_cta.description: Button that opens the onboarding page.

onboarding_title.description: Page title of the onboarding page.

onboarding_intro.message: WireGuardExt is installed in your browser, but it needs a small companion application (the “host”) to route traffic through WireGuard. This is a one-time setup and does not require administrator privileges.

onboarding_step1_title.message: 1. Download the host package for your system

onboarding_step1_desc.message: Go to the latest release page and download the package that matches your operating system.

onboarding_step2_desc_linux.message: Open a terminal in the download folder and run: tar xzf wireguardext-host-linux-amd64-*.tar.gz

onboarding_step2_desc_mac.message: Open a terminal in the download folder and run: tar xzf wireguardext-host-darwin-*.tar.gz

onboarding_step2_desc_windows.message: Right-click the downloaded .zip and choose “Extract all…”.

onboarding_step3_desc_linux_mac.message: In the same folder, run: ./install.sh — it will ask for this extension's ID (shown below).

onboarding_step3_desc_windows.message: Right-click install.ps1 and choose “Run with PowerShell”. It will ask for this extension's ID (shown below).

onboarding_extid_label.message: Your extension ID (paste it when the installer asks):

onboarding_extid_hint.message: If the field above is empty, open chrome://extensions, enable Developer mode and copy the ID shown under WireGuardExt.

onboarding_step4_desc.message: Go back to chrome://extensions and click the reload icon on WireGuardExt, or simply close and reopen the popup. You're ready to go!

onboarding_status_ok.message: ✓ Host connected. You can close this page and use the extension.

onboarding_status_missing.message: The host still can't be reached. Double-check steps 1–4 and reload this page.

profiles.description: Section heading for the list of profiles.

empty_hint.message: You don't have any profiles yet. Create one with "+ New profile" or import a .conf.

fallback_profile.description: Fallback filename when a profile has no name.
